constraints: No live api.z.ai or opencode.ai calls
No .env, config.yaml, or auth material created

commandsRun.observation: All targeted Z.AI executor tests passed, covering non-stream POST/header/body/custom headers/response, streaming SSE usage/[DONE]/errors/body closure paths, empty-key no-dispatch, and local-only CountTokens.
All targeted Z.AI thinking provider tests passed, verifying enabled/disabled mappings, reasoning_effort removal, and clear_thinking preservation.

Static inspection found httptest-only executor tests, outbound Authorization/custom header assertions, tool fields, stream_options.include_usage, [DONE], response Body.Close handling, CountTokens, and Z.AI thinking clear_thinking checks.
Static scan found no executable live provider calls; matches were httptest srv.URL http.Get calls and the Z.AI default base URL constant.
Secret-oriented scan showed placeholder/fake test keys and generic scripts only; no real-looking Z.AI credentials were required or introduced.

assertions.reason: Targeted mocked executor test confirms non-streaming Z.AI execution POSTs to /chat/completions, sets bearer authorization and JSON content headers, forwards custom header:X-ZAI-Test, translates OpenAI chat request/response locally, and uses httptest only.

Mocked streaming tests passed for SSE POST path, Accept text/event-stream, stream_options.include_usage, translated chunks, [DONE], upstream 429 propagation, and implementation closes response bodies on success/error paths.
Thinking provider and executor tests passed: reasoning_effort high maps to thinking.type enabled, none maps to disabled, reasoning_effort is removed, and thinking.clear_thinking is preserved.

Mocked non-streaming executor test includes tools and tool_choice in the outbound body and asserts the function tool name and tool_choice function name are preserved after translation/thinking normalization.
Validation used only local Go tests/static checks. Static scan found httptest usage and no executable live api.z.ai/opencode.ai call; no .env, config.yaml, or auth material was created.

Mocked executor tests confirm custom header attributes header:X-ZAI-Test and header:X-ZAI-Stream are sent outbound. Static inspection shows custom headers come from header: attributes and request logging masks sensitive headers/auth values; secret scan found fake placeholders only.

CountTokens targeted test passed with an httptest server that fails on any request, confirming local-only token counting; implementation translates to OpenAI format, applies Z.AI thinking normalization, counts locally, and returns deterministic usage JSON.

summary: Tested 7 assigned Z.AI executor/thinking assertions: 7 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked.
